questions.question: The CL-604 fuselage is described as a:
questions.explanation: The PTG describes the CL-604 fuselage as a semimonocoque structure.

questions.question: The fuselage is made up of three main sections:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the fuselage consists of nose, center, and tail sections.

questions.question: The nose section includes:

options.A: Main landing gear and cabin only
options.B: Nose landing gear and the flight compartment
options.D: APU and two air conditioning packs

questions.explanation: The PTG states the nose section includes the nose landing gear and the flight compartment.

questions.question: The wing (containing the main landing gear) is bolted to the fuselage:

options.A: Forward of the forward avionics bay

questions.explanation: The PTG states the wing is bolted to the fuselage behind the avionics bay.

questions.question: The tail section is described as:

options.A: Pressurized and used as a passenger area
options.B: Unpressurized and enclosing an equipment bay, supporting engines and empennage
options.C: Pressurized and housing the forward avionics bay

questions.explanation: The PTG describes the tail section as unpressurized, enclosing an equipment bay and supporting engines and the empennage.

questions.question: The PTG notes the weather radar antenna pedestal is:

options.B: On the front bulkhead, covered by a Kevlar radome with conductive paths for lightning protection
options.D: In the tailcone above the ELT

questions.explanation: The PTG states the weather radar pedestal is on the front bulkhead and is covered by a Kevlar radome with conductive paths for lightning protection.

questions.question: The PTG states the airplane has:

options.A: One landing light in each wing tip
options.B: Dual landing lights in the radome
options.D: One landing light in the tailcone

questions.explanation: The PTG states there are dual landing lights installed in the radome.

questions.question: The center section underfloor area is divided into:

options.B: Pressurized avionics bay, unpressurized main landing gear bay, and aft underfloor pressurized area
options.C: Fuel bay, oxygen bay, and radar bay

questions.explanation: The PTG divides the underfloor area into a pressurized avionics bay, an unpressurized main landing gear bay, and an aft underfloor pressurized area.

questions.question: The tail section includes a tail cone consisting of:

options.A: Weather radar antenna and oxygen bottle
options.B: Emergency Locator Transmitter (ELT) and tail fuel tank

questions.explanation: The PTG states the tail cone contains the ELT and the tail fuel tank.

questions.question: The airplane is equipped with which doors/exits (as listed)?

options.A: Only passenger door and cargo door
options.B: Passenger door, cargo/baggage door, aft equipment bay door, and an emergency exit
options.C: Passenger door and two cargo doors only
options.D: Only emergency exit and service doors

questions.explanation: The PTG lists passenger door, cargo/baggage door, aft equipment bay door, and an emergency exit.

questions.question: The passenger door is classified as:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies the passenger door as a Type I emergency exit.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit is classified as:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies the overwing emergency exit as Type III.

questions.question: The PTG shows the passenger door size as approximately:
questions.explanation: The door/exit figure lists the passenger door size as 91 × 178 cm.

questions.question: The PTG shows the overwing emergency exit size as approximately:
questions.explanation: The door/exit figure lists the overwing exit size as 51 × 91 cm.

questions.question: The PTG lists an Oxygen Service Door among the service panels located externally.

options.D: Only when using DC external power

questions.explanation: The service doors/panels figure includes an Oxygen Service Door.

questions.question: Which of the following is shown as a service access/panel location?
questions.explanation: The PTG service panel figure includes the APU/Ground-Air panel.

questions.question: Which antenna is listed/shown on the airplane antenna layout?

options.B: TCAS directional and TCAS omnidirectional antennas

questions.explanation: The antenna layout figure shows TCAS directional and TCAS omnidirectional antennas.

questions.question: The antenna layout includes which items?
questions.explanation: The antenna layout explicitly labels GPS 1 and GPS 2.

questions.question: The EICAS message PASSENGER DOOR means:

options.A: Only the cargo/baggage door is open
options.B: Two or more of the latch/handle sensors indicate the passenger door is not locked and safe
options.C: The passenger door is confirmed locked
options.D: The oxygen service door is open

questions.explanation: The PTG states PASSENGER DOOR indicates 2+ latch/handle sensors show the passenger door is not locked and safe.

questions.question: The EICAS message EXTERNAL DOOR OPEN can indicate that an external service door is open, such as:

options.B: AC ground power door, oxygen refill door, refuel door, external DC ground power door, toilet service door, water-fill door, or aft equipment bay door

questions.explanation: The PTG lists these external doors/panels under EXTERNAL DOOR OPEN.

questions.question: The PTG describes the passenger cabin as:

options.D: Pressurized only when APU is running

questions.explanation: The PTG indicates the center section contains the pressurized passenger cabin.

questions.question: The aft equipment bay is located:

options.B: In the tail section (unpressurized tail section area)

questions.explanation: The PTG describes an equipment bay within the unpressurized tail section.

questions.question: The radome is made of Kevlar and includes conductive paths primarily to:
questions.explanation: The PTG states conductive paths in the Kevlar radome are intended to reduce lightning strike damage.

questions.question: The weather radar antenna pedestal is located on the:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the weather radar antenna pedestal is located on the front bulkhead.

questions.question: The wing is bolted to the fuselage:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the wing is bolted to the fuselage behind the avionics bay.

questions.question: The main landing gear bay is described as:
questions.explanation: The PTG describes the main landing gear bay as unpressurized.

questions.question: Which combination correctly describes the center underfloor areas?

options.B: Pressurized avionics bay + unpressurized MLG bay + aft underfloor pressurized area
options.C: Unpressurized avionics bay + pressurized MLG bay

questions.explanation: The PTG divides the underfloor into pressurized avionics, unpressurized MLG bay, and aft underfloor pressurized area.

questions.question: The passenger door is approximately:
questions.explanation: The PTG door figure lists passenger door size as 91 × 178 cm.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit is approximately:
questions.explanation: The PTG door figure lists overwing exit size as 51 × 91 cm.

questions.question: The cargo/baggage door is approximately:
questions.explanation: The PTG door figure lists the cargo/baggage door size as 109 × 84 cm.

questions.question: The EICAS message CARGO DOOR indicates:

options.B: Two or more latch/handle sensors indicate the cargo door is not locked and safe
options.C: Only the passenger door is open

questions.explanation: The PTG uses the same 2+ sensors not locked/safe logic for CARGO DOOR.

questions.question: The EICAS message PASSENGER DOOR indicates:

options.B: Two or more latch/handle sensors indicate the passenger door is not locked and safe
options.C: Only the emergency exit is open

questions.explanation: The PTG states PASSENGER DOOR indicates 2+ latch/handle sensors show the door is not locked and safe.

questions.question: The EICAS message PAX DOOR OUT HNDL indicates:

options.A: The passenger door is fully closed
options.B: The passenger door outside handle is not stowed
options.C: The cargo door handle is not stowed
options.D: The oxygen service door is open

questions.explanation: The PTG states PAX DOOR OUT HNDL indicates the passenger door outside handle is not stowed.

questions.question: The EICAS message PAX DOOR STOW indicates:

options.A: Passenger door handle is fully extended
options.B: The passenger door is not stowed (door not properly stowed/latched in the open/stow configuration as monitored)
options.D: The cargo door is open in flight

questions.explanation: The PTG includes PAX DOOR STOW as a door stow/position condition monitored by the door sensing system.

questions.question: The EICAS message EXTERNAL DOOR OPEN can indicate an open:

options.B: Service door/panel (e.g., AC ground power, oxygen refill, refuel, external DC power, toilet service, water-fill, aft equipment bay door)

questions.explanation: The PTG lists external service doors/panels under EXTERNAL DOOR OPEN.

questions.question: Which pair of antennas are explicitly shown on the PTG antenna layout?
questions.explanation: The PTG antenna layout labels TCAS directional and TCAS omnidirectional antennas.

questions.question: The PTG antenna layout shows:

options.D: GPS antennas only on the tail

questions.explanation: The PTG antenna layout shows two GPS antennas labeled GPS 1 and GPS 2.

questions.question: Why is the main landing gear bay unpressurized?

options.B: Because it forms part of the structural/door volume for the retractable landing gear, which is not part of the pressure vessel
options.D: Because it houses the oxygen bottle

questions.explanation: The PTG identifies the MLG bay as unpressurized and separate from the pressurized sections; this is consistent with a gear bay outside the pressure vessel.

questions.question: A key safety point of the door sensing logic (“2 or more sensors”) is:

options.A: It guarantees the door is always open
options.B: It adds redundancy so a single sensor fault is less likely to cause a false indication
options.C: It disables EICAS messages in flight
options.D: It only works on the ground

questions.explanation: The PTG’s 2+ sensor logic implies redundancy to reduce false alerts from a single sensor fault.

questions.question: Overall, the “Airplane General” chapter focuses primarily on:

options.B: Structural sections, external panels/doors, and aircraft layout items (radome, bays, antennas) plus related EICAS door indications

questions.explanation: This chapter centers on airframe layout, doors/panels, antennas, and EICAS door indications.

questions.question: The aircraft overall length is approximately:
questions.explanation: Figure 1-1 lists overall length as 68 ft 5 in (20.86 m).

questions.question: The maximum fuselage diameter is approximately:
questions.explanation: Figure 1-1 lists maximum fuselage diameter (width) as 8 ft 10 in (2.69 m).

questions.question: Cabin interior length is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cabin interior dimensions show length as 28 ft 3 in.

questions.question: Cabin interior headroom (floor to max height) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cabin interior dimensions list headroom as 6 ft 1 in.

questions.question: Cabin volume is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cabin interior dimensions list cabin volume as 1150 cu ft.

questions.question: Cabin floor area is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cabin interior dimensions list floor area as 202.5 sq ft.

questions.question: Cabin floor width is approximately:
questions.explanation: Cabin interior dimensions list floor width as 7 ft 2 in.

questions.question: Which of the following items is listed as being contained in the aft equipment bay (tail section)?

options.B: Two batteries and two battery chargers

questions.explanation: The PTG list for the aft equipment bay includes two batteries and two battery chargers.

questions.question: The tail cone consists of:

options.A: The oxygen bottle and refuel panel
options.B: Emergency Locator Transmitter (ELT) and tail fuel tank
options.C: Main landing gear and brake accumulators
options.D: Weather radar antenna and dual landing lights

questions.explanation: The PTG states the tail cone consists of the ELT and the tail fuel tank.

questions.question: Which of the following is shown as an external service panel/access location?
questions.explanation: The PTG service panels/access figure shows an Oxygen Service Door.

questions.question: The PTG service panel figure includes:
questions.explanation: The service panels/access figure explicitly shows the APU/Ground-Air panel.

questions.question: Which fueling-related item is shown in the service panels/access figure?

options.B: Single-Point Pressure Fueling Adapter and Refueling Control
options.D: Tail dump valve service panel only

questions.explanation: The PTG service panels/access figure shows the Single-Point Pressure Fueling Adapter and the Refueling Control.

questions.question: Which electrical ground power access is shown in the service panels/access figure?

options.B: Both AC External Power and DC External Power
options.D: No external power access is shown

questions.explanation: The service panels/access figure shows both AC External Power and DC External Power.

questions.question: The antenna layout includes which two GPS antennas?

options.A: GPS A and GPS B only

questions.explanation: The antenna layout figure labels GPS 1 and GPS 2.

questions.question: Which TCAS antennas are shown on the antenna layout?
questions.explanation: The antenna layout figure shows both TCAS Directional and TCAS Omnidirectional antennas.

questions.question: Which of the following is shown on the antenna layout?
questions.explanation: The antenna layout includes Radio Altimeter 1 and Radio Altimeter 2.

questions.question: Which communications antenna is shown on the antenna layout?

options.B: VHF 1 (also VHF 2 and VHF 3 are shown)
options.C: Only HF long wire (no VHF shown)

questions.explanation: The antenna layout figure shows VHF 1, VHF 2, and VHF 3 antennas.

questions.question: Which of the following items is listed on the “AIRSPEED LIMITS – (INDICATED SPEEDS)” panel in the General Arrangement illustration?
questions.explanation: The airspeed limits panel includes VFE (45 FLAPS).

questions.question: The airspeed limits list includes:

options.B: VFE (multiple flap settings) and VA (maneuvering) with conditions shown

questions.explanation: The list includes multiple VFE entries and VA (maneuvering) with conditions.

questions.question: Overall, the “Airplane General” chapter primarily provides:

options.B: Airframe layout, basic dimensions, doors/exits, service access points, antenna locations, and general cockpit arrangement references

questions.explanation: The chapter provides dimensions, doors/exits, service panels, antenna layout, and general arrangement references.

questions.question: The CL-604 air conditioning system provides temperature-regulated conditioned air to the cockpit and cabin using:
questions.explanation: The guide states the system uses two air conditioning units (ACUs), commonly referred to as packs.

questions.question: Aircraft pressurization is primarily accomplished by regulating the overboard discharge of conditioned air through:
questions.explanation: Pressurization is achieved by controlling outflow via two outflow valves.

questions.question: During unpressurized flight, cockpit and cabin ventilation may be provided by:
questions.explanation: The guide states ram air may be used to ventilate the cockpit and cabin during unpressurized flight.

questions.question: The air conditioning system converts which engine bleed air stage into conditioned air?
questions.explanation: The system converts 10th-stage bleed air into temperature-regulated pressurized air.

questions.question: Bleed air for the packs can come from the engines, the APU, or:
questions.explanation: The guide states bleed air can be supplied by engines, APU, or an external source.

questions.question: A single pack can provide sufficient air to:

options.D: Operate only in manual pressurization mode

questions.explanation: The guide notes one pack can provide sufficient air to cool and pressurize the aircraft.

questions.question: The packs are located in the:
questions.explanation: The left and right packs are located in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The prime function of the packs is to:

options.A: Produce hot air for windshield anti-ice
options.D: Create vacuum for the outflow valves

questions.explanation: The guide states the prime function of the packs is to produce cold air.

questions.question: Pack cold air is produced by cooling hot bleed air with heat exchangers and:

options.B: An air cycle machine (cold air unit)

questions.explanation: Each pack uses heat exchangers and an air cycle machine (cold air unit) to produce cold air.

questions.question: The packs produce temperature-regulated conditioned air by mixing pack cold air with:
questions.explanation: Conditioned air is produced by mixing cold pack air with precooled 10th-stage bleed air.

questions.question: In normal operation, the right pack normally operates from:
questions.explanation: The guide states the right pack normally operates from right engine bleed air.

questions.question: In normal operation, the right pack primarily supplies:
questions.explanation: The right pack normally supplies the passenger cabin.

questions.question: In normal operation, the left pack normally supplies:
questions.explanation: The left pack normally supplies the flight compartment and the passenger cabin.

questions.question: In the event of a single-pack failure, the remaining pack can supply:

options.B: Both compartments with sufficient conditioned air
options.D: Only cabin cooling but not cockpit

questions.explanation: The remaining pack can supply sufficient conditioned air to both compartments.

questions.question: The left pack is powered by:
questions.explanation: The guide states the left pack is powered by the DC Essential Bus.

questions.question: The right pack is powered by:
questions.explanation: The guide states the right pack is powered by DC Bus 2.

questions.question: After a double-generator failure airborne (air-driven generator deployed), which pack remains operational to support high-altitude flight?
questions.explanation: Because it is powered by the DC Essential Bus, the left pack remains operational with ADG deployment.

questions.question: When a PACK switch/light is pressed IN, the corresponding pressure-regulating shutoff valve allows bleed air from the:
questions.explanation: The ACU valve allows bleed air from the 10th-stage manifold to enter the pack.

questions.question: ACU valve LOW mode is used when:

options.A: Bleed air is supplied by the APU on the ground
options.B: Bleed air is supplied by engines and both packs operate

questions.explanation: LOW mode: engine bleed air with both packs operating.

questions.question: ACU valve HIGH mode is used when:

options.A: Airborne during single-pack operation OR on the ground with APU bleed
options.B: Both packs operate on engine bleed
options.C: Cabin is unpressurized and RAM-AIR is open

questions.explanation: HIGH mode occurs for single-pack airborne operation or ground operation with APU supplying bleed air.

questions.question: In LOW mode, the ACU valves regulate pressure to approximately:
questions.explanation: LOW mode regulation is 24 psi.

questions.question: In HIGH mode, the ACU valves deliver approximately:
questions.explanation: HIGH mode delivery is 39.5 psi.

questions.question: If a pack fails for reasons other than overpressure or overtemperature, the failed pack switch/light must be:

options.A: Left ON to enable high mode
options.B: De-selected so the remaining pack can operate in high mode

questions.explanation: The guide notes the failed pack must be de-selected to allow the remaining pack to go to high mode.

questions.question: Pack overtemperature monitoring uses a temperature sensor located:

options.B: Between ACM compressor and secondary heat exchanger

questions.explanation: The sensor is in the ducting between the compressor outlet and the secondary heat exchanger.

questions.question: A pack overtemperature trip causes the pack to shut down by closing the:
questions.explanation: Overtemperature shuts the pack down by closing the pressure-regulating ACU valve.

questions.question: When a pack overtemperature shutdown occurs, which EICAS caution message is displayed?
questions.explanation: Overtemperature triggers L (R) PACK HI TEMP.

questions.question: Pack overpressure monitoring is accomplished by an overpressure switch located:

options.A: In the output of the primary heat exchanger
options.B: At the cabin pressure sense port

questions.explanation: The overpressure switch is in the output of the primary heat exchanger.

questions.question: During a pack overpressure condition, which valves will also close automatically?

options.B: 10th-stage isolation valve and applicable 10th-stage shutoff valve

questions.explanation: Overpressure closes the isolation valve and the applicable 10th-stage shutoff valve, in addition to the ACU valve.

questions.question: A pack overpressure shutdown produces which EICAS caution message?
questions.explanation: Overpressure triggers L (R) PACK HI PRESS.

questions.question: Pack discharge air temperature is controlled by the low-limit valve to keep ACM output:
questions.explanation: The low-limit valve maintains ACM output just above freezing.

questions.question: After the low-limit valve, the air passes through a water separator to:

options.B: Remove water vapor from the cold air

questions.explanation: The water separator removes water vapor from the cold air.

questions.question: Each temperature controller subsystem includes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Altitude limiters are part of the pressurization/outflow valves, not the pack temperature controller subsystem.

questions.question: The cabin fan sensor temperature signal is used for which EICAS status page readout?
questions.explanation: The cabin fan sensor provides the CABIN TEMP display on the EICAS status page.

questions.question: In NORM mode, automatic temperature control uses inputs from:

options.A: Fan sensor, duct sensor, and temperature selector

questions.explanation: NORM mode uses fan sensor, duct sensor, and selector inputs.

questions.question: In STBY mode, the fan sensor is inhibited and the controller uses:
questions.explanation: STBY mode inhibits the fan sensor and uses duct sensor plus selector input.

questions.question: The NORM mode selectable temperature control range is approximately:
questions.explanation: NORM mode range is 15°C to 32°C (60°F to 90°F).

questions.question: The STBY mode selectable temperature control range is approximately:
questions.explanation: STBY mode range is 2°C to 85°C (35°F to 180°F).

questions.question: In NORM mode, when there is more than a 3°F difference between actual and selected temperature, the temperature control valve is commanded to:
questions.explanation: If the error exceeds 3°F, the valve is driven full hot or full cold.

questions.question: In NORM mode, when actual temperature is within 3°F of selected, duct temperature is controlled by:
questions.explanation: Within 3°F, the controller modulates the temperature control valve.

questions.question: In MAN mode, the flight crew directly commands the temperature control valve using:
questions.explanation: Manual mode allows direct control of the temperature control valve via the temperature selector.

questions.question: Ram air is taken from an air scoop located:

options.B: In the base of the vertical fin
options.D: In the main landing gear bay

questions.explanation: The ram air scoop is in the base of the vertical fin.

questions.question: Ram air is used as the cooling medium for the pack heat exchangers and is discharged through exhaust louvers on:

options.B: The upper left and right sides of the aft fuselage

questions.explanation: The guide states discharge is through louvers on the upper left and right sides of the aft fuselage.

questions.question: During ground operations, when ram airflow is inadequate for cooling, which valve opens automatically to draw ambient air from the aft equipment bay?
questions.explanation: The ejector valve opens automatically on the ground when airflow is insufficient.

questions.question: The ejector valve may also be commanded open in flight if:
questions.explanation: It may open in flight if insufficient pack cooling is detected.

questions.question: If both packs are inoperative, the ram air valve may be opened to ventilate the aircraft. The RAM-AIR switch/light is located on:
questions.explanation: The RAM-AIR switch/light is located on the AIR CONDITIONING panel.

questions.question: When the ram air valve is opened, which legend illuminates on the RAM-AIR switch/light?
questions.explanation: The white OPEN legend illuminates when the ram air valve is open.

questions.question: During unpressurized cruise using ram air ventilation, the recommended airspeed is approximately:
questions.explanation: The guide recommends 250 KIAS to provide sufficient airflow to passengers.

questions.question: Cold air from the right pack is ducted (via a check valve on the aft pressure bulkhead) to:

options.B: Passenger positions along the right cabin and to the cockpit

questions.explanation: Right pack cold air is routed along the right cabin positions and to the cockpit.

questions.question: Cold air is also routed below the cabin floor primarily to:
questions.explanation: Cold air is drawn from ducts and routed below the floor to cool avionics.

questions.question: Crossover ducts allow:

options.A: Only cockpit conditioned air to be shut off
options.B: A cold air supply to both sides of the cabin, cockpit, and avionics cooling from one pack if necessary
options.D: Automatic opening of outflow valves above 9.0 psid

questions.explanation: Crossover ducts provide redundancy so one pack can supply both sides and avionics cooling if needed.

questions.question: To meet higher cabin airflow demand, the cabin receives:

options.A: 100% of left pack conditioned air and 40% of right pack conditioned air
options.B: 100% of right pack conditioned air and 40% of left pack conditioned air
options.C: 60% of right pack conditioned air and 40% of left pack conditioned air
options.D: Only bleed air bypass (no pack output)

questions.explanation: The cabin receives 100% of the right pack’s conditioned air and 40% of the left pack’s conditioned air.

questions.question: The cockpit normally receives approximately what portion of the left pack output?
questions.explanation: The remaining 60% of the left pack’s output is ducted to the cockpit.

questions.question: Balancing valves in the conditioned air ducts are used to balance airflow between:

options.B: Left and right cabin sides and between cabin and cockpit

questions.explanation: Balancing valves balance airflow left/right and cabin vs cockpit.

questions.question: The cockpit heat system maintains heater outlet temperature approximately between:
questions.explanation: The duct temperature sensor controls the heating element to maintain 38°C to 49°C (100°F to 120°F).

questions.question: The cockpit heat fan is mounted:

options.B: Under the left-hand side of the cockpit floor

questions.explanation: The fan is mounted under the left-hand side of the cockpit floor.

questions.question: Cockpit heat fan speed protection: if the fan fails to attain at least 60% rated speed within 2 seconds, the system will:

options.B: Shut down the fan and heater
options.C: Open the ram air valve automatically
options.D: Switch to automatic pressurization fail mode

questions.explanation: If the fan does not reach ≥60% within 2 seconds, both fan and heater shut down.

questions.question: During cockpit heat operation, if fan speed drops below 60% for more than 2 seconds, which EICAS status message is displayed?
questions.explanation: A fan speed drop below 60% for >2 seconds triggers CKPT HEAT FAN FAIL.

questions.question: Cockpit heater overtemperature protection: if heater element temperature reaches approximately 65°C, the system will:

options.A: Deactivate the heater while the fan continues and then auto-reset as it cools
options.B: Deactivate both heater and fan permanently

questions.explanation: At ~65°C the thermal safety switch deactivates the heater; the fan continues and the system resets automatically as it cools.

questions.question: Cabin pressurization outflow valves are described as:

options.B: Dual, redundant, electropneumatic, poppet-type valves (primary and secondary)

questions.explanation: The system uses dual redundant electropneumatic poppet-type outflow valves identified as primary and secondary.

questions.question: In automatic mode, the CPC modulates pressurization by regulating the vacuum applied to:

options.A: The secondary outflow valve, with primary slaved
options.B: The primary outflow valve, with secondary slaved

questions.explanation: In automatic mode, CPC controls vacuum to modulate the primary outflow valve; the secondary is slaved to the primary.

questions.question: The CL-604 pneumatic system consists of:

options.B: Two separate bleed air systems: 10th-stage and 14th-stage
options.C: Three bleed air systems: 8th, 10th, and 14th stage
options.D: A hydraulic and a pneumatic system combined

questions.explanation: The pneumatic system consists of two separate bleed air systems: 10th-stage and 14th-stage.

questions.question: 10th-stage bleed air may be supplied from:

options.B: Engines, APU, or external air source

questions.explanation: The 10th-stage bleed air is supplied from the engines, the APU, or an external air source.

questions.question: 14th-stage bleed air may be supplied from:
questions.explanation: The 14th-stage bleed air is supplied from the engines only.

questions.question: 10th-stage bleed air is used for:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage bleed air is used for engine starting and air conditioning/pressurization.

questions.question: 14th-stage bleed air is used for:

options.B: Wing anti-ice, cowl anti-ice, and thrust reversers

questions.explanation: 14th-stage bleed air is used for wing anti-ice, cowl anti-ice, and thrust reversers.

questions.question: The 10th-stage bleed air manifold is located in the:
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage bleed air manifold is located in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The 14th-stage bleed air manifold is located in the:
questions.explanation: The 14th-stage bleed air manifold is located in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The 10th-stage isolation valve divides the 10th-stage system into:
questions.explanation: An isolation valve divides the 10th-stage system into left and right manifolds.

questions.question: With the 10th-stage isolation valve closed, the left manifold may be pressurized from:

options.B: External air, APU, or left engine bleed air

questions.explanation: With the isolation valve closed, the left manifold may be pressurized by external air, APU, or left engine bleed air.

questions.question: With the 10th-stage isolation valve closed, the right manifold is normally pressurized by:
questions.explanation: With the isolation valve closed, the right engine bleed air normally pressurizes the right manifold.

questions.question: One-way check valves in the 10th-stage bleed ducts prevent:

options.B: Reverse flow of manifold air into the engines

questions.explanation: The check valves prevent reverse flow of manifold air into the engines.

questions.question: Similar one-way check valves are also used for the:

options.A: APU and external ground air connector
options.C: Ram air valve and outflow valve

questions.explanation: Similar check valves are used for the APU and the external ground air connector.

questions.question: 10th-stage manifold pressure readings are derived from:

options.B: Two pressure transducers, one for each manifold side

questions.explanation: There are two pressure transducers: one for the left manifold and one for the right manifold.

questions.question: 10th-stage bleed pressure readouts are displayed on the:
questions.explanation: The pressure readings for the left and right 10th-stage manifold are displayed on the EICAS Status page.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL valve is:
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage isolation valve is electrically controlled and pneumatically operated.

questions.question: When no 10th-stage bleed air is available, the 10TH STAGE ISOL valve is spring-loaded:
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage isolation valve is spring-loaded closed when no 10th-stage bleed air is available.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL valve is also spring-loaded closed when:

options.C: The aircraft is on the ground

questions.explanation: The valve is spring-loaded closed when electrical control power is lost.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL switch/light manually commands the valve:
questions.explanation: The switch/light manually commands the isolation valve open or closed.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL valve is automatically commanded open during:
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage isolation valve opens automatically during engine starts.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL valve is automatically commanded closed when:
questions.explanation: The isolation valve closes automatically when a pack overpressure occurs.

questions.question: The 10TH STAGE ISOL switch/light indicates valve status by showing:
questions.explanation: Valve status is indicated by the switch/light OPEN annunciation.

questions.question: The engine 10th-stage bleed air valves control the supply of 10th-stage bleed air:

options.B: To the engines for starting and from the engines for bleed supply

questions.explanation: The 10th-stage bleed air valves control supply to the engines for starting and from the engines for bleed supply.

questions.question: The engine 10th-stage bleed air valves are located in the:
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage bleed air valves are located in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The L (R) 10TH STAGE switch/lights manually command the 10th-stage bleed valves:
questions.explanation: The L and R 10TH STAGE switch/lights manually command the respective valves open or closed.

questions.question: The engine 10th-stage bleed air valves are automatically commanded open during:
questions.explanation: They are automatically commanded open during engine starts (starter engagement).

questions.question: The engine 10th-stage bleed air valves are automatically commanded closed when:

options.A: The associated ENG FIRE PUSH switch/light is pressed

questions.explanation: They close automatically when the associated ENG FIRE PUSH is pressed and also when a pack overpressure occurs.

questions.question: 10th-stage valve status is indicated by the respective switch/light annunciation:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage valve status is indicated by the CLOSED annunciation.

questions.question: At full thrust, 10th-stage bleed air temperature can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage bleed air temperature can reach 700°F (370°C) at full thrust.

questions.question: At full thrust, 10th-stage bleed air pressure can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage bleed air pressure can reach 170 psi at full thrust.

questions.question: The APU Load Control Valve (LCV) controls APU bleed air supply to the:
questions.explanation: The APU LCV controls APU bleed air supply to the 10th-stage manifold.

questions.question: The APU LCV is:
questions.explanation: The APU LCV is an electrically controlled, pneumatically operated valve.

questions.question: When the APU LCV switch/light is pressed, the valve opens and allows APU bleed air to enter the:
questions.explanation: When opened, the APU LCV allows APU bleed air to enter the left bleed air manifold.

questions.question: The APU LCV is automatically commanded closed by:
questions.explanation: The APU LCV closes automatically for excessive APU EGT or APU LCV interlock protection.

questions.question: APU bleed air temperature can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: APU bleed air temperature can reach 500°F (260°C).

questions.question: APU bleed air pressure can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: APU bleed air pressure can reach 50 psi.

questions.question: The APU LCV interlock provides a second level of protection by preventing manifold pressure from entering the:
questions.explanation: The APU LCV interlock prevents manifold pressure from entering the APU compressor.

questions.question: The APU ECU monitors the switch positions of the LCV, the 10th-stage isolation valve, and the:
questions.explanation: The APU ECU monitors the LCV, 10th-stage isolation valve, and left/right 10th-stage bleed switches.

questions.question: The APU LCV will automatically close or be prevented from opening whenever:

options.A: The L 10th-stage bleed switch/light is pressed open
options.B: Both the R 10th-stage bleed switch/light and ISOL switch/light are pressed open
options.D: The DUCT MON selector is in TEST

questions.explanation: The interlock closes or prevents opening if the L 10th-stage is open, or if both the R 10th-stage and ISOL are open.

questions.question: The external ground air connector is located:

options.A: On the nose below the radome
options.B: On the aft fuselage below the left engine

questions.explanation: The external ground air connector is located on the aft fuselage below the left engine.

questions.question: Ground air from the external connector may be supplied to the:
questions.explanation: Ground air may be supplied to the left manifold of the 10th-stage bleed air system.

questions.question: 14th-stage bleed air can only be supplied by:
questions.explanation: The guide states 14th-stage bleed air can only be supplied by an operating engine.

questions.question: 14th-stage manifold check valves are installed to prevent:

options.B: Reverse flow of 14th-stage air to an operating engine

questions.explanation: Manifold check valves prevent reverse flow of 14th-stage air to an operating engine.

questions.question: The 14th-stage bleed air valves are:
questions.explanation: The 14th-stage bleed air valves are electrically controlled and pneumatically operated.

questions.question: When no 14th-stage bleed air is available, the 14th-stage valves are spring-loaded:
questions.explanation: The 14th-stage bleed air valves are spring-loaded open when no 14th-stage bleed air is available.

questions.question: The 14th-stage valves are also spring-loaded open when:

options.C: The aircraft is on the ground

questions.explanation: The valves are spring-loaded open when electrical control power is lost.

questions.question: The 14th-stage bleed air valves are automatically commanded closed when:

options.A: The associated ENG FIRE PUSH switch/light is pressed
options.B: The DUCT MON selector is held in TEST

questions.explanation: The valves are automatically commanded closed when the associated ENG FIRE PUSH switch/light is pressed.

questions.question: After engine N2 spool down removes bleed air, a 14th-stage valve will reopen:

options.A: Only if the ENG FIRE PUSH switch/light is reset
options.B: Regardless of ENG FIRE PUSH switch/light position

questions.explanation: When bleed air is no longer available after engine N2 spool down, the valve reopens regardless of ENG FIRE PUSH position.

questions.question: 14th-stage valve status is indicated by the respective switch/light annunciation:
questions.explanation: 14th-stage valve status is indicated by the CLOSED annunciation.

questions.question: At full thrust, 14th-stage bleed air temperature can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: 14th-stage bleed air temperature can reach 950°F (510°C) at full thrust.

questions.question: At full thrust, 14th-stage bleed air pressure can reach approximately:
questions.explanation: 14th-stage bleed air pressure can reach 215 psi at full thrust.

questions.question: The bleed air leak detection system monitors pneumatic ducting downstream of the 10th-stage and 14th-stage bleed air shutoff valves and:
questions.explanation: The system monitors ducting downstream of the shutoff valves and wing anti-ice valves.

questions.question: Bleed air leaks between the engine and the engine bleed air shutoff valves are detected by the:

options.B: Engine and jet pipe/pylon overheat detection system

questions.explanation: Leaks between the engine and bleed air shutoff valves are detected by the engine and jet pipe/pylon overheat detection system.

questions.question: The 10th-stage manifold uses a bleed leak detection system of what type?
questions.explanation: The 10th-stage manifold uses a dual-loop sensing system.

questions.question: On the 10th-stage dual-loop leak detection system, a duct overheat warning occurs when:

options.C: The DUCT MON selector is in LOOP A

questions.explanation: On a dual-loop system, both loops must detect a leak before a warning is given.

questions.question: The 14th-stage bleed air manifold and center fuselage wing anti-icing ducts are monitored by:
questions.explanation: The 14th-stage manifold and center fuselage wing anti-icing ducts are monitored by single-loop systems.

questions.question: Thermal switches are used in the leading edges of the wings to detect:

options.B: Uncontrolled leakage of anti-icing piccolo tubes

questions.explanation: Thermal switches in the wing leading edges detect uncontrolled leakage of anti-icing piccolo tubes.

questions.question: A bleed leak in the left 10th-stage duct will produce which EICAS warning message?
questions.explanation: A left 10th-stage duct leak produces the L 10TH DUCT warning.

questions.question: A bleed leak in the left or right 10th-stage or 14th-stage duct also causes the aural warning:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage and 14th-stage duct leak warnings trigger the “BLEED AIR DUCT” aural warning.

questions.question: A bleed leak in the fuselage or wing anti-ice ducting causes which EICAS warning message?
questions.explanation: A leak in the fuselage or wing anti-ice ducting causes the ANTI-ICE DUCT warning.

questions.question: Selecting and holding the DUCT MON selector to TEST verifies all sensing elements by simulating a bleed air leak. Successful completion also displays:
questions.explanation: A successful DUCT MON TEST results in the DUCT TEST OK advisory EICAS message.

questions.question: The Challenger 604 is equipped with two:

options.B: General Electric CF34-3B high-bypass turbofan engines

questions.explanation: The CL-604 is equipped with two General Electric CF34-3B high-bypass ratio turbofan engines.

questions.question: Normal takeoff thrust rating is approximately:
questions.explanation: Normal takeoff thrust rating is 8,729 pounds per engine.

questions.question: During engine-out operation, APR increases thrust on the operating engine to approximately:
questions.explanation: During engine-out operation, APR increases thrust on the operating engine to 9,220 pounds.

questions.question: The CF34-3B is flat-rated to:
questions.explanation: The engine is flat-rated to ISA +15°C at sea level.

questions.question: The engine has two independently rotating major assemblies identified as:

options.A: N1 fan and N2 compressor rotor
options.B: LP spool and HP spool only

questions.explanation: The engine consists of a fan rotor (N1) and a compressor rotor (N2).

questions.question: The N1 rotor consists of a single-stage fan connected to a:
questions.explanation: The N1 rotor is a single-stage fan connected through a shaft to a four-stage low-pressure turbine.

questions.question: The N2 rotor is a:

options.A: 10-stage compressor connected to a four-stage turbine
options.B: 12-stage compressor connected to a two-stage turbine
options.C: 14-stage axial-flow compressor connected to a two-stage high-pressure turbine
options.D: 16-stage compressor connected to a single-stage turbine

questions.explanation: The N2 rotor is a 14-stage axial-flow compressor connected to a two-stage high-pressure turbine.

questions.question: The accessory gearbox is mechanically driven by the:
questions.explanation: The accessory gearbox is mechanically driven by the N2 compressor.

questions.question: Bypass airflow produces approximately how much of takeoff thrust?
questions.explanation: Bypass airflow produces approximately 80% of the thrust at takeoff.

questions.question: Jet pipe (core exhaust) thrust produces approximately how much of takeoff thrust?
questions.explanation: Jet pipe thrust produces approximately 20% of the takeoff thrust.

questions.question: The N1 fan consists of:
questions.explanation: The N1 fan is a single-stage fan consisting of 28 titanium blades.

questions.question: A Kevlar blanket around the inlet housing is provided to:

options.B: Contain damage from a failed fan blade

questions.explanation: A Kevlar blanket is wrapped around the inlet housing to contain damage from a failed fan blade.

questions.question: The variable geometry compressor system regulates airflow by changing the position of:

options.B: The inlet guide vanes and first five stages of stator vanes
options.D: The turbine nozzle guide vanes only

questions.explanation: The VG system changes the position of the compressor inlet guide vanes and the first five stages of stator vanes.

questions.question: Fuel metered by the FCU is used hydraulically to:

options.C: Open the thrust reverser blocker doors

questions.explanation: Fuel metered by the FCU is used to hydraulically change the vane angle in the VG system.

questions.question: The accessory gearbox drives all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: The accessory gearbox drives lubrication pumps, alternator for N1 control amplifier, hydraulic pump, fuel pump, and IDG. It does not drive the thrust reverser PDU.

questions.question: The engine-driven fuel pump is mounted on the:
questions.explanation: The engine-driven fuel pump is mounted on the accessory gearbox.

questions.question: The engine-driven fuel pump assembly contains:

options.B: Two pump elements in one housing
options.C: Three separate pumps in one housing
options.D: A boost pump and a scavenge pump only

questions.explanation: The engine-driven fuel pump is comprised of three separate pumps contained within a single housing.

questions.question: Engine fuel is used for all of the following EXCEPT:

options.D: Directly powering the air turbine starter

questions.explanation: Fuel is used for VG control, oil heat exchange, FCU servos, and motive flow for ejectors, but not to power the ATS.

questions.question: Combustion fuel can be interrupted by moving the thrust lever to SHUTOFF or by selecting:
questions.explanation: Fuel can be interrupted by moving the thrust lever to SHUTOFF or by selecting the engine FIRE PUSH switch/light.

questions.question: Moving the thrust lever to SHUTOFF stops fuel flow at the:
questions.explanation: The SHUTOFF position shuts off the fuel at the FCU.

questions.question: Selecting ENG FIRE PUSH stops engine fuel by closing the:
questions.explanation: The FIRE PUSH switch/light closes the engine fuel shutoff valve.

questions.question: The fuel/oil heat exchanger is used to:

options.A: Cool the fuel and warm the oil
options.B: Warm the fuel and cool the oil

questions.explanation: The fuel/oil heat exchanger warms engine fuel and cools engine oil.

questions.question: If the fuel filter becomes clogged, the system:

options.B: Bypasses the filter to ensure continued operation
options.D: Switches to manual fuel control only

questions.explanation: If the filter becomes clogged, fuel bypasses the filter to ensure continued engine operation.

questions.question: A clogged fuel filter is indicated by which EICAS caution message?
questions.explanation: A clogged filter is indicated by the L(R) FUEL FILTER caution message.

questions.question: At low power settings (N1 below 79%), engine speed is controlled primarily in:
questions.explanation: At low power settings (N1 below 79%), the FCU hydromechanically controls N2 speed.

questions.question: At high thrust settings (N1 above 79%), engine speed is controlled primarily in:

options.B: N1 mode by the ECU/N1 control amplifier

questions.explanation: At higher thrust settings, the ECU (N1 control amplifier) trims the FCU fuel output to achieve the desired N1 rpm.

questions.question: Automatic switchover from N2 to N1 speed governing occurs at approximately:
questions.explanation: With ENG SPEED selected ON, automatic switchover occurs at 79% N1.

questions.question: If an ENG SPEED switch is moved from ON to OFF at high power settings, the engine will revert to:
questions.explanation: Moving ENG SPEED from ON to OFF at high power reverts the engine to N2 speed control.

questions.question: A rapid increase in engine acceleration and possible overtemperature may occur if:

options.B: ENG SPEED is moved from ON to OFF at high power
options.D: The thrust lever is retarded to idle

questions.explanation: The guide cautions that moving ENG SPEED from ON to OFF at high power can cause rapid acceleration and possible overtemperature.

questions.question: APR arms during takeoff when the APR switch is selected to ARM and:
questions.explanation: APR is armed during takeoff when APR is selected ARM and both engines are above 79% N1.

questions.question: APR arming is indicated by which advisory message?
questions.explanation: APR arming is indicated by the APR ARM advisory message.

questions.question: During takeoff, APR monitors both engines and activates when either engine drops below approximately:
questions.explanation: APR activates if an N1 drop below 67.6% is sensed on either engine.

questions.question: When APR activates, the normally operating engine increases N1 by a minimum of about:
questions.explanation: APR increases N1 fan speed by a minimum of 2%.

questions.question: APR activation provides approximately how much additional thrust?
questions.explanation: The increase in rpm equates to approximately 500 pounds of thrust.

questions.question: When APR is activated, a green APR icon appears:

options.A: In the ITT gauge of both engines
options.B: In the center of the N1 gauge of the operating engine
options.C: Next to the thrust lever position

questions.explanation: A green APR icon appears in the center of the N1 gauge of the operating engine.

questions.question: Normal APR test operation results in which advisory message?
questions.explanation: A successful APR test produces APR TEST 1, 2, or 3 OK.

questions.question: If APR system test fails, which caution message is displayed?
questions.explanation: If the APR system fails its test, APR INOP is displayed.

questions.question: Maximum oil consumption is approximately:
questions.explanation: Maximum oil consumption is 6.4 ounces or 0.05 US gallons per hour.

questions.question: Engine oil pressure is monitored redundantly by a:

options.B: Pressure switch and separate pressure transmitter

questions.explanation: A pressure switch and a separate pressure transmitter are used to monitor oil pressure.

questions.question: Low oil pressure detected by the pressure switch generates which warning?
questions.explanation: Low oil pressure detected by the switch produces the L(R) ENG OIL PRESS warning.

questions.question: Chip detector and impending oil filter bypass indications are located at:

options.C: JB-5 in the aft equipment bay

questions.explanation: Chip detector and impending bypass indications are provided on Junction Box 5 in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: Oil replenishment tank capacity is approximately:
questions.explanation: The oil replenishment system includes a replenishment tank holding approximately 6.0 U.S. quarts.

questions.question: Engine oil levels should normally be checked:

options.B: Between 15 minutes and two hours after shutdown

questions.explanation: Oil levels should be checked between 15 minutes and two hours after engine shutdown.

questions.question: Maximum refill allowable before dry-motoring the engine is:
questions.explanation: Maximum refill allowable is 2 U.S. quarts before dry-motoring is required.

questions.question: Engine bleed air is extracted from which compressor stages?
questions.explanation: Engine bleed air is extracted from the 7th, 10th, and 14th stages.

questions.question: 7th-stage bleed air is used for:

options.C: Pressurization of oil seals and venting of engine sumps

questions.explanation: 7th-stage bleed air is used for pressurization of oil seals and venting of engine sumps.

questions.question: 10th-stage bleed air is used for:
questions.explanation: 10th-stage bleed air is used for the air conditioning/pressurization systems and engine starting.

questions.question: 14th-stage bleed air is used for:
questions.explanation: 14th-stage bleed air supplies engine cowl and wing anti-ice or thrust reverser systems.

questions.question: The engine starting system consists of the starter control valve and the:
questions.explanation: The starting system consists of the starter control valve and the air turbine starter (ATS).

questions.question: To open the starter control valve and engage the ATS, the system requires:
questions.explanation: DC electrical power and air from the 10th-stage bleed manifold are required.

questions.question: Minimum bleed pressure for an APU-assisted engine start is approximately:
questions.explanation: Minimum APU bleed pressure for start is 40 psi.

questions.question: Minimum bleed pressure for an external air cart start is approximately:
questions.explanation: Minimum external air cart pressure for start is 45 psi.

questions.question: Minimum bleed pressure for an engine crossbleed start is approximately:
questions.explanation: Minimum crossbleed pressure is 60 psi (approximately 85% N2).

questions.question: During a normal start, the ATS remains engaged until approximately:
questions.explanation: During a normal start, the starter remains engaged until 55% N2.

questions.question: The ATS is capable of dry-motoring the engine to approximately:
questions.explanation: The starter is capable of dry-motoring the engine up to approximately 28% N2.

questions.question: Which statement about starter re-engagement is correct?

options.A: The engine must be fully stopped before re-engagement
options.B: The ATS may be engaged at any rpm up to 55% N2
options.C: The ATS may only engage below 28% N2

questions.explanation: The starter clutch does not require complete stop; the ATS may be engaged up to 55% N2.

questions.question: During engine start, the white START light indicates that the:
questions.explanation: When the starter control valve opens, a white light illuminates in the associated START switch/light.

questions.question: Normal ignition is energized:

options.A: Whenever ARM is selected, regardless of starter engagement
options.B: Upon starter engagement and de-energized at starter cutout

questions.explanation: In normal mode, ignition is energized upon starter engagement and de-energized at starter cutout (55% N2).

questions.question: Ignition system A is powered by the:

options.A: Battery Bus through a static inverter

questions.explanation: Ignition system A is powered by the AC Essential Bus.

questions.question: Ignition system B is powered by the:

options.B: Battery Bus through a static inverter

questions.explanation: Ignition system B is powered by the Battery Bus through a static inverter.

questions.question: The CL-604 APU installed is a:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies the installed APU as a GTCP-36-100E constant-speed gas turbine APU.

questions.question: The APU is installed in a fire-resistant compartment in the:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU is installed in a fire-resistant compartment in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The APU generator primarily provides AC electrical power during:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU generator provides AC electrical power during ground operations.

questions.question: In flight, the APU serves as a:
questions.explanation: The APU serves as a backup AC power source in flight.

questions.question: The APU also provides pressurized bleed air to the:
questions.explanation: The APU provides pressurized bleed air to the 10th-stage manifold for air conditioning and engine starting.

questions.question: The maximum operating altitude of the APU is:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the maximum operating altitude of the APU is 20,000 feet MSL.

questions.question: In-flight APU starting is guaranteed below:
questions.explanation: The PTG states in-flight APU starting is guaranteed below 15,000 feet.

questions.question: In-flight APU starting has been demonstrated up to:
questions.explanation: The PTG states in-flight APU starting has been demonstrated up to 20,000 feet.

questions.question: In-flight APU starts have been demonstrated between approximately:
questions.explanation: The PTG states in-flight starts have been demonstrated from 141 to 290 KIAS.

questions.question: In-flight APU bleed air extraction for air conditioning is not permitted above:
questions.explanation: The PTG states in-flight APU bleed air extraction for air conditioning is not permitted above 14,500 feet.

questions.question: The APU enclosure is best described as a:
questions.explanation: The APU enclosure is described as a stainless-steel, fireproof box.

questions.question: The APU enclosure includes a service door for:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the enclosure is equipped with a service door for APU oil servicing.

questions.question: A spring-loaded flapper door on the left side of the APU enclosure provides:

options.B: cooling airflow while the APU is operating

questions.explanation: The flapper door provides cooling airflow while the APU is operating.

questions.question: When the APU is shut down, the enclosure flapper door is:
questions.explanation: When the APU is shut down, the flapper door is spring-loaded closed.

questions.question: The APU power section consists of a gas turbine engine with integrated:

options.C: anti-ice, fuel dump, and APR systems
options.D: bleed air, vacuum, and cabin fan systems

questions.explanation: The PTG states the power section consists of a gas turbine engine with integrated oil, ignition, and start systems.

questions.question: The APU power section drives a gearbox that reduces rotational speed for operation of:
questions.explanation: The gearbox reduces APU rotational speed to a speed appropriate for gearbox-mounted accessories.

questions.question: The APU major components include a single-stage centrifugal compressor and a:
questions.explanation: The APU includes a single-stage centrifugal compressor and a single-stage turbine.

questions.question: The APU combustor is an:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies the combustor as an annular reverse flow combustor.

questions.question: APU power is developed through compression of ambient air by the compressor, then mixed with fuel and ignited in the combustor to drive the:
questions.explanation: The compressed air, mixed with fuel and ignited, drives a turbine.

questions.question: The rotating shaft of the turbine rotor drives the compressor and the drive shaft of the:
questions.explanation: The turbine rotor drives the compressor and the drive shaft of the accessory gearbox.

questions.question: The accessory gearbox provides power transfer from the starter motor during start and also powers gearbox-mounted accessories during:
questions.explanation: The accessory gearbox powers accessories during on-speed rotation.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a gearbox-mounted accessory?
questions.explanation: Listed accessories include start motor, generator adapter, speed sensor, lubrication module, and FCU. Not the outflow valve actuator.

questions.question: The APU gearbox also serves as:

options.B: an oil reservoir and wet-sump lubrication system

questions.explanation: The gearbox serves as an oil reservoir and wet-sump lubrication system.

questions.question: APU gearbox oil cooling is achieved by:

options.B: airflow over cooling fins on the lower gearbox casing
options.C: ram air through the cabin floor

questions.explanation: Gearbox oil is cooled by airflow over cooling fins integral to the lower casing of the accessory gearbox.

questions.question: After APU shutdown, wait a minimum of how long before checking gearbox wet-sump oil level?
questions.explanation: The PTG says wait a minimum of 30 minutes for gearbox oil level to stabilize.

questions.question: The generator adapter transfers rotational power from the gearbox to the:
questions.explanation: The generator adapter transfers APU rotational power from the gearbox to the APU generator.

questions.question: The generator adapter has a:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the generator adapter has a dedicated pressurized lubrication system.

questions.question: Generator adapter oil is cooled by a:
questions.explanation: The generator adapter oil is cooled by a fuel/oil heat exchanger.

questions.question: Cooling fuel for the generator adapter heat exchanger normally comes from the:
questions.explanation: The cooling fuel supply to the heat exchanger is from the normal APU fuel feed from the right main tank.

questions.question: If normal APU fuel feed is not available, generator adapter oil will:

options.A: continue cooling normally through a backup line
options.B: not be cooled, limiting APU operating time
options.C: be cooled by the gearbox fins only with no limitation
options.D: automatically transfer to the left tank heat exchanger

questions.explanation: If normal fuel feed is unavailable, generator adapter oil will not be cooled and APU operating time will be limited.

questions.question: After APU shutdown, wait a minimum of how long before checking generator adapter oil level?
questions.explanation: The PTG says to wait a minimum of 30 minutes for generator adapter oil level to stabilize.

questions.question: Before opening the APU generator/adapter oil filler cap after shutdown, you should:

options.A: open it immediately while oil is warm
options.B: bleed excess pressure by pushing the pressure-relief valve cap for at least 5 seconds
options.D: turn on the APU fuel pump

questions.explanation: The PTG cautions to bleed excess pressure for at least 5 seconds before opening the filler cap.

questions.question: The APU generator is rated at:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies a 30 kVA generator.

questions.question: The APU generator produces:
questions.explanation: The PTG identifies it as a 30 kVA, 400-Hz, three-cycle AC generator.

questions.question: The APU generator is:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU generator is identical to the engine-driven generators.

questions.question: If the APU generator is used to supply electrical power in flight, bleed air extraction from the APU is:

options.D: permitted only with one pack off

questions.explanation: The PTG states that if the APU generator is supplying electrical power in flight, APU bleed air extraction is not permitted.

questions.question: Use of the APU generator in flight is not permitted unless:

options.B: both main generators have failed or are off-line

questions.explanation: The PTG notes APU generator use in flight is only permitted if both main generators have failed or are off-line.

questions.question: The APU air intake is located:

options.A: on the right lower aft fuselage
options.B: on the upper left aft fuselage near the base of the vertical stabilizer

questions.explanation: The air intake is on the upper left portion of the aft fuselage near the base of the vertical stabilizer.

questions.question: The opening of the APU intake is:

options.C: covered by a movable inlet door

questions.explanation: The PTG states the intake opening is flush with the fuselage.

questions.question: APU exhaust gases exit on the:

options.A: left side of the aft fuselage below the left engine pylon
options.B: right side of the aft fuselage below the right engine pylon

questions.explanation: The exhaust duct exits on the right side of the aft fuselage below the right engine pylon.

questions.question: The APU intake duct is an:

options.B: S-shaped duct with inlet screen and water trap
options.D: axial straight duct with bypass door

questions.explanation: The intake is an S-shaped duct with an inlet screen and a water trap with drain line.

questions.question: The APU exhaust duct muffler assembly also acts as an ejector, drawing cooling air through the enclosure by means of the:
questions.explanation: The muffler assembly acts as an ejector, drawing cooling air through the spring-loaded flapper door.

questions.question: APU exhaust gas temperature is indicated on the:
questions.explanation: APU EGT is indicated on the EICAS status page.

questions.question: The normal fuel supply to the APU comes from the:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the normal fuel supply to the APU is from the right main tank.

questions.question: A backup fuel supply is provided from the left engine motive flow system in case:

options.D: the ram air valve is open

questions.explanation: Backup fuel from the left engine motive flow system is provided for negative-G conditions.

questions.question: The APU fuel feed system includes an APU fuel pump, an APU fuel shutoff valve, a negative-G shutoff valve, one-way check valves, and a:
questions.explanation: The PTG lists the APU FCU as part of the APU fuel feed system.

questions.question: The APU fuel pump is powered by the:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU fuel pump is powered by the DC Battery Bus.

questions.question: Selecting the APU PWR FUEL switch/light activates the APU fuel pump and:

options.A: opens the APU fuel SOV and negative-G SOV
options.D: connects the APU generator to the AC buses

questions.explanation: Selecting PWR FUEL activates the pump and opens the APU fuel SOV and negative-G SOV.

questions.question: Insufficient APU fuel pump output pressure is indicated by the PUMP FAIL light and which EICAS caution message?
questions.explanation: Insufficient pump pressure gives the PUMP FAIL indicator and the APU PUMP caution.

questions.question: Fuel not used for combustion is routed through the fuel/oil heat exchanger to cool:
questions.explanation: The remaining fuel is routed through the fuel/oil heat exchanger to cool generator adapter oil.

questions.question: The APU fuel shutoff valve opens when the PWR FUEL switch/light is:
questions.explanation: The APU fuel shutoff valve opens when the PWR FUEL switch/light is pressed in.

questions.question: If the APU fuel shutoff valve does not match the PWR FUEL switch position within 5 seconds, which EICAS caution appears?
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU SOV caution appears if the valve does not match switch position within 5 seconds.

questions.question: When the APU FIRE PUSH switch/light is pressed, the APU fuel shutoff valve and the negative-G shutoff valve will:
questions.explanation: APU FIRE PUSH commands both valves closed.

questions.question: Successful closing of the APU fuel shutoff and negative-G shutoff valves after APU FIRE PUSH is indicated by:

options.B: the green SOV CLOSED light and APU SOV CLOSED advisory

questions.explanation: Successful closing is indicated by the green SOV CLOSED light and the APU SOV CLOSED advisory.

questions.question: The APU negative-G shutoff valve allows fuel from the left engine feed line to automatically supply the APU FCU during:

options.B: negative-G conditions or APU fuel pump failure

questions.explanation: The negative-G SOV allows alternate fuel supply during negative-G conditions or APU fuel pump failure.

questions.question: A one-way check valve in the negative-G system prevents alternate fuel from entering the:
questions.explanation: A one-way check valve prevents this alternate fuel from entering the fuel/oil heat exchanger.

questions.question: If the APU negative-G shutoff valve fails to close after APU FIRE PUSH, which EICAS caution is displayed?
questions.explanation: Failure of the negative-G shutoff valve to close triggers APU NEG-G SOV.

questions.question: The APU fuel control unit is driven by the:
questions.explanation: The PTG states the APU FCU is driven by the accessory gearbox.

questions.question: The APU Electronic Control Unit (ECU) is powered by the Battery Bus when:

options.A: the START/STOP switch is pressed only
options.B: the APU PWR FUEL switch/light is pressed in
options.C: the APU AVAIL light is illuminated
options.D: the APU generator is selected on-line

questions.explanation: The ECU is powered by the Battery Bus when the APU PWR FUEL switch/light is pressed in.

questions.question: In the APU start sequence, ignition is activated at 10% RPM, starter cutout occurs at 60%, ignition is deactivated at 95%, and the green AVAIL light illuminates at approximately:
questions.explanation: The PTG start sequence states AVAIL illuminates at 95% + approximately 4 seconds.

questions.question: The Challenger 604 primarily uses:
questions.explanation: The aircraft primarily uses 115-volt AC power and 28-volt DC power.

questions.question: The primary source of AC electrical power during normal operations is provided by:
questions.explanation: Two engine-driven integrated drive generators (IDGs) supply the primary AC power source.

questions.question: The APU generator provides AC power primarily when:

options.A: The engines are operating normally in cruise
options.B: The aircraft is on the ground with the engines off

questions.explanation: The APU generator provides AC electrical power on the ground with the engines off, and may also be used in flight after failure of both engine generators.

questions.question: If total AC power is lost in flight, the emergency AC source is the:
questions.explanation: In flight, a total AC power loss causes the ADG to provide emergency AC power.

questions.question: External AC electrical power is connected through a receptacle located on the:

options.A: left side of the aft fuselage
options.B: right side of the forward fuselage

questions.explanation: External AC electrical power is supplied through a receptacle on the right side of the forward fuselage.

questions.question: DC electrical power is primarily supplied by:
questions.explanation: DC electrical power needs are primarily supplied by four TRUs.

questions.question: The two NiCad batteries are located in the:
questions.explanation: The main and APU NiCad batteries are located in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The aircraft accepts 28-volt DC external power through a receptacle installed:

options.A: on the rear fuselage below the right engine
options.B: on the forward fuselage below the nose

questions.explanation: The DC external receptacle is installed on the rear fuselage below the right engine.

questions.question: How many circuit breaker panels provide electrical power distribution?
questions.explanation: There are five circuit breaker panels: four in the flight compartment and one in the aft equipment bay.

questions.question: The electrical system is controlled from the ELECTRICAL POWER panel located on the:

options.B: left side of the overhead panel

questions.explanation: Control and operation are accomplished through the ELECTRICAL POWER panel on the left side of the overhead panel.

questions.question: Each engine-driven IDG supplies:
questions.explanation: Each IDG supplies 115-volt AC, 400-Hz, three-phase electrical power.

questions.question: An IDG consists of:

options.B: A constant-speed drive and an electrical generator
options.D: An AC generator and a hydraulic pump

questions.explanation: The IDG consists of a constant-speed drive and an electrical generator.

questions.question: The nominal rating of each engine-driven generator is:

options.C: 30 kVA to 35,000 feet, then 25 kVA to 41,000 feet

questions.explanation: Each generator is rated at 30 kVA to 35,000 feet MSL and 25 kVA to 41,000 feet MSL.

questions.question: In the event of a single engine generator failure, the remaining generator is capable of:

options.A: Powering only the AC ESS bus
options.B: Powering only one main AC bus
options.C: Supplying sufficient power for operation of the main systems

questions.explanation: The remaining generator can supply sufficient power for operation of the main systems.

questions.question: Selecting GEN 1 or GEN 2 to OFF/RESET will:

options.B: Disconnect the respective generator and reset its control circuit
options.D: Transfer the AC ESS bus to AC BUS 2

questions.explanation: OFF/RESET disconnects the respective generator from the AC buses and can reset the generator control circuit after a fault.

questions.question: The generator control unit (GCU) will trip an engine generator off-line for all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: The GCU trips the generator for over/undervoltage, over/underfrequency, and generator or bus overcurrent.

questions.question: The APU generator is rated at:
questions.explanation: The APU generator nominal rating is 30 kVA up to the APU's maximum operating altitude.

questions.question: The maximum operating altitude of the APU generator is:
questions.explanation: The APU generator is rated to the APU maximum operating altitude of 20,000 feet MSL.

questions.question: Use of the APU generator in flight is permitted only if:

options.A: Both engine generators are on line
options.B: The APU is the preferred source over the IDGs
options.C: Both main generators have failed or are off-line

questions.explanation: The APU generator may not be selected ON in flight unless both GEN 1 and GEN 2 are off-line or failed.

questions.question: The APU generator is removed from the AC buses for:

options.A: Over/undervoltage, over/underfrequency, and generator or bus overcurrent

questions.explanation: The APU GCU trips the APU generator off-line for over/undervoltage, over/underfrequency, and generator or bus overcurrent.

questions.question: When connected and within limits, external AC power is indicated by which light on the EXT POWER switch/light?
questions.explanation: The green AVAIL annunciator illuminates when external AC power is within voltage, frequency, and phase limits.

questions.question: Pressing the EXT POWER switch/light with AVAIL illuminated will:

options.A: Connect external AC power to the main AC buses
options.B: Connect external DC power to the battery bus
options.D: Transfer the AC ESS bus to ADG power

questions.explanation: Pressing the switch with AVAIL illuminated connects external AC power to the main AC buses via the auxiliary power/external power contactor.

questions.question: The aircraft AC bus system consists of:
questions.explanation: The AC system has five buses: AC BUS 1, AC BUS 2, AC ESS BUS, AC UTILITY BUS 1, and AC UTILITY BUS 2.

questions.question: AC BUS 1 normally powers:

options.B: AC ESS BUS and AC UTILITY BUS 1

questions.explanation: AC BUS 1 normally supplies AC ESS BUS and AC UTIL BUS 1.

questions.question: AC BUS 2 normally powers:
questions.explanation: AC BUS 2 normally powers AC UTIL BUS 2.

questions.question: The normal AC bus priority logic for AC BUS 1 or 2 is:

options.A: External AC, APU generator, cross-side engine generator, on-side engine generator
options.B: On-side engine generator, APU generator, cross-side engine generator, external AC power
options.C: APU generator, external AC, on-side generator, cross-side generator
options.D: ADG, on-side generator, APU generator, external AC

questions.explanation: Main AC bus priority is on-side engine generator first, then APU generator, then cross-side engine generator, then external AC power.

questions.question: Selecting an AUTOXFER switch/light to OFF will:

options.A: Disable automatic bus transfer for that main AC bus
options.D: Reset the AC ESS transfer logic

questions.explanation: AUTOXFER OFF inhibits automatic bus tie operation for the corresponding main AC bus.

questions.question: The white OFF annunciation on an AUTOXFER switch/light indicates:

options.B: Automatic bus tie operation has been manually disabled
options.D: The ADG is powering the AC ESS bus

questions.explanation: The white OFF annunciator shows the flight crew has manually disabled automatic bus transfer.

questions.question: An amber FAIL annunciation on an AUTOXFER switch/light indicates:

options.A: The switch has been selected OFF
options.B: Automatic bus transfer has been automatically inhibited due to a bus fault or generator overcurrent
options.C: External AC power is out of limits
options.D: The AC utility buses are shed

questions.explanation: FAIL indicates automatic bus tie logic has been disabled because of a bus fault or generator overcurrent.

questions.question: The AC ESS bus is normally powered by:
questions.explanation: The AC ESS bus is normally powered by AC BUS 1.

questions.question: If AC BUS 1 loses power, the AC ESS BUS is automatically transferred to:
questions.explanation: Loss of AC BUS 1 causes the AC ESS bus to transfer automatically to AC BUS 2.

questions.question: When the AC ESS bus is powered by AC BUS 2, which EICAS status message appears?
questions.explanation: AC ESS ALTN indicates the AC ESS bus is being supplied from AC BUS 2.

questions.question: The AC utility buses supply power to:
questions.explanation: AC UTILITY BUS 1 and 2 supply AC power to nonessential cabin equipment.

questions.question: During single-generator operation in flight, AC UTILITY BUS 1 and 2 are:

options.D: Powered only if flaps are extended

questions.explanation: During single-generator operation in flight, both AC utility buses are load shed to reduce electrical load.

questions.question: During ground operations, AC utility buses are shed when:

options.B: Single generator or external AC is supplying power and the flaps are not retracted
options.D: The BATT MASTER switch is OFF

questions.explanation: On the ground, AC utility buses are shed when a single generator or external AC is supplying power and the flaps are not retracted.

questions.question: The ADG provides emergency AC power to the:
questions.explanation: The ADG provides power to the AC ESS bus.

questions.question: In addition to the AC ESS bus, the ADG also powers:
questions.explanation: The ADG provides 115-volt AC power to the AC ESS bus and hydraulic pump 3B.

questions.question: The ADG is located:

options.B: In the forward right fuselage beside the nose gear

questions.explanation: The ADG is located in the forward right fuselage beside the nose gear.

questions.question: Automatic ADG deployment occurs when:

options.A: Either AC BUS 1 or AC BUS 2 becomes unpowered on the ground
options.B: Both AC BUS 1 and AC BUS 2 become unpowered and the aircraft is in flight
options.C: The BATT MASTER switch is OFF

questions.explanation: Automatic deployment requires both AC BUS 1 and AC BUS 2 to be unpowered and the aircraft to be in flight.

questions.question: Another prerequisite for automatic ADG deployment is that:

options.A: At least one main generator was on line before both main AC buses went off
options.B: The APU generator was on line before the failure
