questions.explanation: Each bottle in the dual-bottle system holds 2603 liters (115 cubic feet).

questions.question: In the dual-bottle system, the flight compartment and passenger oxygen storage bottles are located in the:
questions.explanation: Both dual-system oxygen bottles are located in the forward avionics bay.

questions.question: Oxygen for the flight crew is provided through a:
questions.explanation: Flight crew oxygen is supplied through a diluter demand regulating system.

questions.question: A quick-donning, inflatable-harness oxygen mask is installed:

options.B: in a container at each side console

questions.explanation: A quick-donning mask is installed in a container at each side console.

questions.question: The portable oxygen bottle is normally located:

options.B: behind the pilot in a placarded position

questions.explanation: The portable oxygen bottle is normally located in a placarded position behind the pilot.

questions.question: Passenger oxygen masks are installed above all passenger seats and in the:
questions.explanation: Dropdown passenger masks are installed above all passenger seats and in the lavatory.

questions.question: Passenger oxygen masks are of the:
questions.explanation: Passenger oxygen masks are fixed-dilution, free-flow type.

questions.question: Passenger oxygen masks are designed for use at cabin pressure altitudes below:
questions.explanation: The passenger masks are designed for cabin pressure altitudes below 25,000 feet.

questions.question: When the passenger oxygen system is active, which EICAS message is displayed?
questions.explanation: When the passenger oxygen system is active, the PAX OXY ON caution message is displayed.

questions.question: Therapeutic oxygen normally supplies oxygen to how many cabin ports?
questions.explanation: Therapeutic oxygen normally supplies four cabin ports.

questions.question: If oxygen storage bottle pressure becomes excessive, oxygen is vented overboard through a relief valve in the:
questions.explanation: Excessive oxygen pressure is vented overboard through a relief valve in the right forward fuselage.

questions.question: In the single-bottle oxygen system, a green frangible disk indicates:

options.B: relief valve activation due to overpressure

questions.explanation: A fragmented green frangible disk indicates the relief valve activated because of overpressure.

questions.question: In the single-bottle system, the relief valve activates when HP module pressure exceeds approximately:
questions.explanation: For the single-bottle system, the relief valve activates when HP module pressure exceeds about 2600 psi.

questions.question: In the single-bottle system, the relief valve also activates when LP module pressure exceeds:
questions.explanation: In the single-bottle system, LP module relief occurs above 130 psi.

questions.question: In the dual-bottle system, one frangible disk ruptures if HP module pressure exceeds approximately:
questions.explanation: In the dual-bottle system, one disk ruptures if HP module pressure exceeds approximately 2800 psi.

questions.question: In the dual-bottle system, the other frangible disk ruptures if LP module pressure exceeds:
questions.explanation: In the dual-bottle system, the LP module disk ruptures at about 90 psi.

questions.question: An amber OXY LO PRESS message is displayed when oxygen pressure falls below:
questions.explanation: The amber OXY LO PRESS message appears when oxygen pressure falls below 800 psi.

questions.question: Pressing the RESET/TEST switch on the flight crew oxygen mask container tests:
questions.explanation: The RESET/TEST switch tests oxygen flow through the regulator.

questions.question: The flow indicator (blinker) shows a yellow cross when:

options.C: the microphone is selected to MASK

questions.explanation: A yellow cross in the blinker indicates oxygen flow.

questions.question: The flow indicator is black when:
questions.explanation: The flow indicator appears black when no oxygen is flowing.

questions.question: When the mask is in use, a white ON flag indicates that the:
questions.explanation: The white ON flag indicates the oxygen shutoff valve is open.

questions.question: To stop oxygen flow to the crew mask after use, the crew should close the container doors and press the:
questions.explanation: Closing the doors and pressing RESET/TEST stops oxygen flow and removes the ON flag.

questions.question: On the crew oxygen mask, selecting NORMAL on the Normal/100% lever provides:

options.B: a diluted mixture of oxygen and ambient air adjusted for cabin altitude

questions.explanation: NORMAL provides a diluted oxygen and ambient air mixture adjusted for cabin altitude.

questions.question: On the crew oxygen mask, selecting 100% provides:
questions.explanation: The 100% position provides pure oxygen.

questions.question: On the Emergency Flow Control knob, NORMAL provides oxygen:

options.C: only when the microphone is active

questions.explanation: In NORMAL, oxygen is regulated on demand.

questions.question: On the Emergency Flow Control knob, EMERGENCY provides:

options.B: a constant flow of oxygen at positive pressure

questions.explanation: EMERGENCY delivers a constant flow of oxygen at positive pressure.

questions.question: The PRESS TO TEST position on the Emergency Flow Control knob is used to:

options.B: purge the mask of smoke or noxious fumes

questions.explanation: PRESS TO TEST purges the mask of smoke or noxious fumes.

questions.question: The microphone in the oxygen mask is activated on the Audio Control Panel by selecting:
questions.explanation: To use the oxygen mask microphone, the MASK/BOOM MIC switch must be set to MASK.

questions.question: To don the quick-donning oxygen mask with one hand, the crew squeezes the red tabs on either side of the oxygen line to:
questions.explanation: Squeezing the red tabs inflates the harness for one-hand donning.

questions.question: The oxygen mask harness is not designed to be donned over:
questions.explanation: The PTG notes that the harness is not designed to be donned over headsets or eyeglasses.

questions.question: Passenger oxygen is controlled from the PASSENGER OXYGEN control panel located on the:
questions.explanation: The PASSENGER OXYGEN control panel is located on the copilot's side console.

questions.question: The PASSENGER OXYGEN control panel has how many operating modes?
questions.explanation: The panel has three modes: CLOSED, NORMAL, and OVERRIDE.

questions.question: In CLOSED, the passenger oxygen control panel:

options.B: stops oxygen supply to the dropdown masks

questions.explanation: CLOSED stops the oxygen supply to the dropdown passenger masks.

questions.question: In NORMAL, passenger dropdown masks deploy when the altimetric valve senses cabin altitude greater than:
questions.explanation: NORMAL deploys the masks when cabin altitude exceeds 10,500 feet or 14,500 feet, depending on aircraft serial number.

questions.question: In OVERRIDE, the passenger oxygen system:

options.A: stops oxygen flow but opens the doors
options.B: deploys the masks and supplies oxygen

questions.explanation: OVERRIDE deploys the masks and immediately supplies oxygen.

questions.question: If passenger dropdown masks do not deploy, the mask box door can be opened by:

options.A: pulling the nearest life vest tab
options.B: inserting a long, thin object into the release hole

questions.explanation: If the masks do not deploy, the mask box door can be opened with a long, thin object inserted into the release hole.

questions.question: Therapeutic oxygen is controlled from the flight compartment by a switch/light on the PASSENGER OXYGEN control panel. Selecting it ON will:

options.B: supply oxygen to the cabin therapeutic ports

questions.explanation: Selecting THERAPEUTIC OXYGEN ON supplies oxygen to the cabin ports for first aid use.

questions.question: The main entrance door is classified as a:
questions.explanation: The main entrance door is classified as a Type I emergency exit.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit is classified as a:
questions.explanation: The overwing emergency exit is a Type III emergency exit.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit is a removable:
questions.explanation: The overwing emergency exit is a removable plug-type hatch.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit provides access to the:
questions.explanation: It provides access to the right upper wing surface.

questions.question: The overwing emergency exit has an escape rope (lifeline) stored:

options.B: to the right of the exit window

questions.explanation: The lifeline is stored to the right of the exit window.

questions.question: The Emergency Egress Lighting System operates for approximately:
questions.explanation: The Emergency Egress Lighting System operates for approximately 15 minutes after activation.

questions.question: The Emergency Locator Transmitter (ELT) is located in the:
questions.explanation: The ELT is located in the tail cone.

questions.question: The ELT is activated automatically by G-forces in the event of a:
questions.explanation: The ELT is activated automatically by impact G-forces in the event of a crash.

questions.question: The ELT transmits on the international emergency frequencies of:
questions.explanation: The ELT transmits on 121.5 and 243.0 MHz.

questions.question: The ELT sealed battery pack provides at least:
questions.explanation: The ELT battery pack provides at least 48 hours of operation.

questions.question: Underwater Locating Devices (ULDs) are part of the:

options.C: passenger oxygen and life raft systems

questions.explanation: The ULDs are part of the Flight Data Recorder and Cockpit Voice Recorder systems.

questions.question: With the ELT ON/ARM switch set to ARM:

options.B: the automatic operating mode is selected

questions.explanation: With the switch in ARM, the automatic mode is selected.

questions.question: The ON position of the ELT switch permits:
questions.explanation: The ON position allows manual ELT operation.

questions.question: When the ELT transmitter is in operation, the ON light on the ELT panel will:
questions.explanation: When the ELT is operating, the ON light illuminates.
